text: Agile methodology is a popular approach to software development that focuses on flexibility and teamwork. Instead of planning everything at the start, teams work in short cycles called sprints, which usually last two weeks. Each day, the team holds a quick standup meeting to discuss progress and challenges. Before a sprint begins, they review the backlog, a list of tasks to be completed. These tasks are written as user stories, which describe features from the end user’s perspective. The key idea is iterative development: after each sprint, the team delivers a working part of the product, then adapts the next steps based on feedback.
